zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Ros於1398年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素時，她的父親Sir William de Ros, Knt.29歲，母親Margaret FitzAlan28歲。她於1414年2月24日與James Touchet 5th Baron Audley在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1430年2月14日在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Ros于1398年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素时，她的父亲Sir William de Ros, Knt.29岁，母亲Margaret FitzAlan28岁。她于1414年2月24日与James Touchet 5th Baron Audley在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1430年2月14日在英国英格兰斯塔福爾享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Ros was born in 1398, in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, her father, Sir William de Ros, Knt., was 29 and her mother, Margaret FitzAlan, was 28. She married James Touchet 5th Baron Audley on 24 February 1414, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 14 February 1430, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Ros est née en 1398, à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ros, Knt., avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margaret FitzAlan, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé James Touchet 5th Baron Audley le 24 février 1414, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 février 1430, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Ros 1398 in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ros, Knt. 29 und ihre Mutter Margaret FitzAlan 28. Sie hat am 24. Februar 1414 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom James Touchet 5th Baron Audley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 14. Februar 1430 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ros è nata il nel 1398 nel Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, sua padre, Sir William de Ros, Knt., aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Margaret FitzAlan, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato James Touchet 5th Baron Audley il 24 febbraio 1414, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 14 febbraio 1430, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Rosが1398年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ros, Knt.は29歳，母Margaret FitzAlanは28歳であった。1414年2月24日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでJames Touchet 5th Baron Audleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1430年2月14日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアで32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Ros(이)가 1398년 영국 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ros, Knt.(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret FitzAlan(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1414년 2월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 James Touchet 5th Baron Audley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1430년 2월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ros nasceu em 1398, em Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ros, Knt., tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Margaret FitzAlan, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com James Touchet 5th Baron Audley em 24 de fevereiro de 1414, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de fevereiro de 1430, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Ros родилась в 1398 г. в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ros, Knt. было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret FitzAlan, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Touchet 5th Baron Audley 24 февраля 1414 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 14 февраля 1430 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Ros nació en 1398, en Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Ros, Knt., tenía 29 años y su mamá, Margaret FitzAlan, tenía 28 años. Se casó con James Touchet 5th Baron Audley el 24 de febrero de 1414, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 14 de febrero de 1430, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 32 años.
